client_information.type_of_assignment: Medical appointment – new patient intake and follow‑up consultation with internal medicine physician, including detailed discussion of recent lab work, medication management, and care coordination.
client_information.special_needs:
Jonathan is a Deaf adult who uses American Sign Language (ASL) as his primary language and reads English, but prefers complex medical information to be conveyed directly in ASL rather than relying only on written notes. He also has some light sensitivity due to migraines, so bright overhead lighting in the exam room can sometimes make it harder for him to focus on the interpreter’s signing. If possible, please seat the interpreter so that Jonathan can see both the doctor and the interpreter clearly without having to twist his neck, ideally with the interpreter next to the physician, facing Jonathan, with minimal visual distractions in the background.

Jonathan mentioned that he sometimes arrives with his 10‑year‑old hearing daughter, but he does NOT want her to be used as an interpreter under any circumstances; he wants a qualified, certified ASL interpreter present for the entire appointment, including check‑in, nursing intake, the exam with the physician, and any follow‑up with the lab or scheduling staff. He is comfortable with either a male or female interpreter but prefers someone experienced with medical terminology, especially related to hypertension, high cholesterol, and migraine management.

He also requested that if there are any forms to be signed (HIPAA releases, consent for treatment, or new‑patient paperwork), the interpreter be available to interpret the content of those forms in ASL before he signs so that he fully understands the documents. Finally, Jonathan has mild carpal tunnel syndrome in his right wrist; if there are extended discussions, he may occasionally pause or adjust his signing hand position, and he appreciates when interpreters maintain a steady, moderate signing pace and check in briefly if he looks confused or asks for clarification.

location_of_services.name_location_of_services: Oakwood Family Health Center – Internal Medicine Clinic
location_of_services.on_site_contacts_name_phone_number: On-site contact: Karen Lee, RN – Charge Nurse, (248) 555-2410
location_of_services.address_location_of_services: 21475 Greenfield Road, Suite 300, Oak Park, MI 48237
location_of_services.directions:
The clinic is located on Greenfield Road just south of 9 Mile Road, in a three‑story brick medical office building labeled "Oakwood Family Health Center." The main entrance faces Greenfield Road, with a blue awning over the doorway. Free patient parking is available in the surface lot on the north side of the building, with additional accessible parking spaces near the front entrance.

Upon entering the building, take the elevator on the right side of the lobby to the 3rd floor. When you exit the elevator, turn left; the Internal Medicine Clinic (Suite 300) is at the end of the hallway on the right. Check in at the front desk and ask for Karen Lee, RN, or let staff know you are the ASL interpreter for Jonathan Harris’s appointment at 9:00 am. Please arrive 10–15 minutes early to allow time for check‑in and to position yourself in the exam room for optimal visibility.

billing_information.address_billing_information: Attn: Billing Department, 21475 Greenfield Road, Suite 100
